## Plan: Three Rivers Bank Business Credit Card Website

Build a React frontend with Spring Boot backend that displays credit cards for small businesses, integrating with BIAN API v13.0.0 read-only endpoints without authentication requirements.

### Steps

1. **Set up project structure** with React frontend (`/frontend`) using Vite, Spring Boot backend (`/backend`) with Maven, and configure `/frontend` with React Router, React Query for server state, Material-UI components, and `/backend` with Spring Web, JPA, H2 Database, Feign Client dependencies (no Spring Security needed for read-only public APIs).

2. **Create H2 database schema** with `@Entity` classes for `CreditCard` (id, name, cardType, annualFee, introApr, regularApr, rewardsRate, signupBonus, creditScoreNeeded, foreignTransactionFee, description, features, benefits), `CardFeature` (id, cardId, featureName, featureValue, featureType), `FeeSchedule` (id, cardId, feeType, feeAmount, feeDescription), and `InterestRate` (id, cardId, rateType, rateValue, effectiveDate, calculationMethod) to store real-time fees and interest data, configure `application.yml` with H2 in-memory database (`spring.datasource.url: jdbc:h2:mem:creditcards`, `spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: create`, `spring.h2.console.enabled: true`), and create `data.sql` to preload 3-5 Three Rivers Bank products with complete fee schedules and interest rates: **Business Cash Rewards** (2% cashback, $0 annual fee, 0% intro 12 months then 18.99%-26.99% APR, $300 signup bonus), **Business Travel Rewards** (3X points travel/dining, $95 annual fee, 19.99%-27.99% APR, 50k points signup bonus), **Business Platinum** (0% intro APR 15 months, $0 annual fee, 20.99%-28.99% APR, no foreign fees), **Business Premium** (unlimited 1.5% cashback, $150 annual fee, 17.99%-25.99% APR, travel insurance, purchase protection), **Business Flex** (tiered 3%-1% rewards, $0 annual fee, 21.99%-29.99% APR, expense tracking tools).

3. **Implement read-only BIAN API integration** by creating `BianApiClient.java` with Feign configured to connect to Swagger Hub mock server (`https://virtserver.swaggerhub.com/B154/BIAN/CreditCard/13.0.0`) for public GET endpoints (`/CreditCard/{id}/Retrieve`, `/CreditCard/{id}/CardTransaction/{txid}/Retrieve`, `/CreditCard/{id}/Billing/{billingid}/Retrieve`), create `CreditCardService.java` that primarily queries H2 database for all catalog data including real-time fees and interest rates with optional BIAN API enrichment for transactions and billing details, implement circuit breaker pattern (Resilience4j) with fallback to H2-only data when BIAN API unavailable, map BIAN response models to DTOs (`CreditCardDto.java`, `CardTransactionDto.java`, `BillingDto.java`), configure retry logic (3 retries, 5s timeout), and add caching with `@Cacheable` for BIAN API responses.

4. **Build frontend UI components** starting with `CardComparisonPage.jsx` showing side-by-side comparison table with filters (annual fee range, rewards type, APR range, card type), `CardDetailsPage.jsx` with hero section showing card image, quick facts card (APR/fees/rewards), detailed features accordion (rates table, rewards breakdown, terms), benefits grid highlighting business perks (expense management, employee cards, QuickBooks integration), and `HomePage.jsx` with featured cards carousel, category navigation, and rewards calculator widget.

5. **Create read-only REST API endpoints** at `/api/cards` (GET all cards with filtering/sorting from H2), `/api/cards/{id}` (GET detailed card info from H2 including fees and interest), `/api/cards/{id}/transactions` (GET sample transactions from BIAN for demo), `/api/cards/{id}/fees` (GET fee schedule from H2), `/api/cards/{id}/interest` (GET interest details from H2), configure Spring Boot Actuator with health check endpoint at `/actuator/health` exposing H2 database status, BIAN API connectivity, and application readiness, enable OpenAPI/Swagger documentation at `/swagger-ui.html`, configure CORS for frontend access, and make H2 console accessible at `/h2-console` for debugging.

6. **Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline** with `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` to build React frontend (npm install, npm run build), build Spring Boot backend (Maven clean package), run JUnit tests with H2 database (`mvn test`), run Playwright UI tests (`npx playwright test`), create Docker images for frontend (Nginx serving static files) and backend (Java 17+ container with H2 embedded), push images to Azure Container Registry, and deploy to Azure Container Apps with environment-specific configurations and automated testing gates.

7. **Implement comprehensive testing** with JUnit 5 for Spring Boot (`CreditCardServiceTest.java` testing H2 queries and BIAN fallback logic, `CreditCardControllerTest.java` with MockMvc, `CreditCardRepositoryTest.java` validating H2 schema and preloaded data, `BianApiClientTest.java` using WireMock for API mocking), Playwright tests for UI in `/tests/e2e/` directory (`card-comparison.spec.ts`, `card-details.spec.ts`, `filters-and-sorting.spec.ts`, `responsive-design.spec.ts`, `database-integration.spec.ts` verifying H2 data loads correctly) with test fixtures in `/tests/fixtures/credit-cards.json` matching H2 preloaded data (5 cards with complete details), visual regression testing with baseline screenshots, accessibility checks (WCAG 2.1 AA), and GitHub Actions configuration to run tests across multiple browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) and viewports (desktop 1920x1080, tablet 768x1024, mobile 375x667).

8. **Brand as Three Rivers Bank** by creating Material-UI custom theme with navy/teal color scheme in `/frontend/src/theme.js`, adding Three Rivers Bank logo to `Header.jsx` and all card designs, creating `BankInfo.jsx` component with contact info (phone: 1-800-THREE-RB, email: business@threeriversbank.com, headquarters: Pittsburgh, PA), adding `Footer.jsx` with branch locator and social media links, and implementing business-specific features section highlighting expense management, QuickBooks integration, employee card controls, and receipt capture mobile app.

### Implementation Decisions

1. **H2 Database Configuration** - Use JPA `ddl-auto: create` with `data.sql` for schema initialization. H2 will store complete card catalog including real-time fees and interest rates. For production transition, maintain schema compatibility with PostgreSQL/MySQL using standard JPA annotations. Add indexes on card name, card type, and annual fee range for optimized queries.

2. **BIAN API Integration** - Use Swagger Hub mock server (https://virtserver.swaggerhub.com/B154/BIAN/CreditCard/13.0.0) for BIAN API calls. Implement Resilience4j circuit breaker pattern with fallback to H2 data when API unavailable. Configure retry logic: 3 attempts, 5 second timeout per request. Cache BIAN responses with TTL: 5 minutes for transactions, 1 hour for billing data.

3. **Data Storage Strategy** - H2 database serves as primary source for all card catalog data including fees and interest rates. BIAN API provides supplementary data for transactions and billing details only. No background sync jobs needed; H2 data is authoritative for card features and pricing.

4. **Azure Container Apps Configuration** - Backend: 0.5 vCPU, 1GB RAM (includes H2 memory allocation). Frontend: 0.25 vCPU, 0.5GB RAM. Azure Container Registry with GitHub service principal for CI/CD. Ingress: HTTPS-only with custom domain support. Environment variables: `BIAN_API_URL`, `H2_CONSOLE_ENABLED=false` (production), `LOGGING_LEVEL=INFO`. Health checks via `/actuator/health` endpoint monitoring H2 connectivity and BIAN API status.

5. **Playwright Test Configuration** - E2E tests organized in `/tests/e2e/` directory. Test fixtures in `/tests/fixtures/credit-cards.json` mirror H2 preloaded data (5 cards with complete details, fees, and interest rates). Baseline screenshots stored in `/tests/screenshots/baseline/` for visual regression. GitHub Actions runs tests across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit browsers with viewports: desktop (1920x1080), tablet (768x1024), mobile (375x667). Parallel execution enabled for faster CI/CD pipeline.
